If you've forgotten your password, use the 'Forgot Password' link or contact your university's ICT helpdesk.
content:
Blackboard Login — South African University Portals

Blackboard Learn is the Learning Management System (LMS) used by many South African universities. This page lists the direct login portals for every major institution so you can get to your coursework as quickly as possible.

Quick Access: All University Blackboard Login Links

UniversityBlackboard Login URLICT Helpdesk
UFS — University of the Free Stateblackboard.ufs.ac.za+27 51 401 9111
VUT — Vaal University of Technologyblackboard.vut.ac.za+27 16 950 9000
UFH — University of Fort Hareblackboard.ufh.ac.za+27 40 602 2011
CPUT — Cape Peninsula University of Technologycput.blackboard.com+27 21 959 6767
UP — University of Pretoriaclickup.up.ac.za+27 12 420 3111
UCT — University of Cape Townvula.uct.ac.za+27 21 650 4500
Wits — University of the Witwatersrandlearn.wits.ac.za+27 11 717 1000
TUT — Tshwane University of Technologyblackboard.tut.ac.za+27 12 382 4911
NWU — North-West Universityefundi.nwu.ac.za+27 18 299 1111
WSU — Walter Sisulu Universityblackboard.wsu.ac.za+27 47 502 2111

How to Login to Blackboard — Step-by-Step Guide

If you're logging in to your university's Blackboard system for the first time, follow these steps:

  1. Go to your university's Blackboard URL — Use the table above to find your institution's direct Blackboard link.
  2. Enter your student number — This is your official student number as issued by your university's registration office.
  3. Enter your password — First-time users typically use their ID number or a temporary password sent to their university email address.
  4. Click Sign In — You will be taken to your Blackboard dashboard where you can access all your modules.
  5. If you cannot see your modules — Wait 24–48 hours after registration for modules to appear. Contact your faculty office if modules are still missing after this time.

Troubleshooting Blackboard Login Problems

Forgotten Password

Click "Forgot Password" on the Blackboard login page. You will receive a password-reset link to your official university email address. If you no longer have access to that email, contact your university's ICT helpdesk in person with your student card.

Account Locked

Accounts are locked after 5 failed login attempts. Wait 30 minutes and try again, or contact your ICT helpdesk to unlock your account immediately.

Browser Issues

Blackboard works best on Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox. Clear your browser cache and cookies (Ctrl+Shift+Delete on PC, Cmd+Shift+Delete on Mac) and try again. Avoid using Internet Explorer.

System Maintenance

Universities perform maintenance on Blackboard during evenings and weekends. Check your university's ICT service page or social media for scheduled downtime announcements.

Struggling with More Than Just a Login? Bursary Assistance for 2026

At StudyBuddy, we know that staying enrolled at university requires more than just internet access — it requires funding. Many South African students lose their place at university due to financial exclusion, not academic failure.

We provide bursary assistance services to help you:

  • ✅ Identify bursaries and student funding you qualify for
  • ✅ Prepare and submit strong bursary applications
  • ✅ Navigate NSFAS appeals and funding queries
  • ✅ Find corporate and sector-specific bursaries (mining, engineering, health, business)
  • ✅ Apply for 2026 bursary cycles before deadlines close

Our services are available to students at all major South African universities including UFS, VUT, UFH, CPUT, TUT, NWU, Wits, UCT, and more.

👉 Browse available 2026 bursaries →

👉 Get StudyBuddy's AI-powered academic support →

⚠️ Disclaimer: StudyBuddy is an independent student support platform. We are not officially affiliated with any South African university or Blackboard Inc. All university trademarks belong to their respective owners. Your data is protected under POPIA and is only used to facilitate bursary assistance.

faqs.question: How do I log in to Blackboard at my South African university?
faqs.answer: Go to your university's Blackboard URL (e.g., blackboard.ufs.ac.za for UFS or blackboard.vut.ac.za for VUT), enter your student number and password, and click Sign In. First-time users typically use their ID number as the default password.

faqs.question: What do I do if I forgot my Blackboard password?
faqs.answer: Click 'Forgot Password' on your university's Blackboard login page. A reset link will be sent to your official university email address. If you cannot access that email, contact your university's ICT helpdesk in person with your student card.

faqs.question: Why can't I see my modules on Blackboard?
faqs.answer: Modules appear on Blackboard 24–48 hours after your registration is processed. If modules are still missing after 48 hours, contact your faculty administrator or the Blackboard support team at your university.

faqs.question: How can I get bursary assistance as a South African university student?
faqs.answer: StudyBuddy provides bursary assistance services to help South African students identify, apply for, and secure funding for the 2026 academic year. We cover NSFAS, corporate bursaries, and sector-specific funding options.

faqs.question: Which South African universities use Blackboard?
faqs.answer: South African universities that use Blackboard Learn include UFS, VUT, UFH, TUT, WSU, and others. UCT uses Vula, Wits uses Learn, NWU uses eFundi, and UP uses ClickUP — all of which are similar learning management systems.
